sanskrit:
एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः।
अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति॥
transliteration:
evaṃ pravartitaṃ cakraṃ nānuvartatīha yaḥ |
aghāyur-indriyārāmo moghaṃ pārtha sa jīvati ||
literal_meaning:
Thus set-in-motion the wheel; whoever does not turn with it here,
lives a sin-lengthening, sense-pleasing life in vain, O Pārtha.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns that opting out of the cooperative ‘wheel’ of mutual contribution (yajña) leaves the person spinning only on the hedonic treadmill; the psyche registers the refusal as chronic guilt (‘sin-life’) and existential emptiness.
psychological_state: Narcissistic hedonism masking covert shame; life feels ‘wasted’ despite surface pleasures.
trigger_scenario: High-earning professional who parties every weekend yet wakes up with a hollow feeling and wonders ‘Is this all there is?’
anchor_text: Join life’s give-and-take or stay stuck in guilt and empty pleasure.
